levels.body: “Decorum” is used to describe someone as decent, proper in behavior and in their character or rank proper to their circumstance (“Decorum, *n*.”, Sense 1.b.).
worksCited: “Decorum, *n*.” *Oxford English Dictionary*, Oxford UP, December 2025, doi.org/10.1093/OED/9352978363. Accessed 18 Mar. 2026.

levels.body: By stating that the raven wore a “grave and stern decorum of the countenance”, the raven is associated with a typically human expression. The phrase, thus, personifies the raven similar to [[mien]] in line 40.
